content: WebRTC topology for game input (not video): Full mesh P2P ideal for 2-4 players — lowest latency (50-150ms), zero server media cost, but O(N^2) connections drains mobile battery past 5 players. SFU appropriate for 5+ players — each device sends once, server forwards without processing, still low latency. For ImmersiveRT specifically: phone→desktop is the only hot path (sensor data). Server relay (via TURN) is the fallback when NAT blocks P2P — TURN costs are low for IMU packets (tiny payload, no audio/video). Recommendation: start with phone↔desktop direct P2P via TURN/STUN signaling; no need for SFU since sensor data is many-to-one (phones→1 desktop), not many-to-many. MCU (mixing) is irrelevant for game input.
